author: bandantonio
description: ️Fastest Apple Books highlights importer. The only one protecting your own reflections across imports. Fully customizable.

manifest.author: bandantonio
manifest.description: Import your Apple Books highlights and notes to Obsidian.
manifest.name: Apple Books - Import Highlights

name: Apple Books - Import Highlights

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- After installation, the default value for the "Template for naming highlight files" setting wasn't recognized as a valid template expression due to an invalid syntax. This caused the plugin to create a single file named "bookTitle" and overwrite it with each book's annotations during import instead of creating separate files for each book based on their titles. ([#78](https://github.com/bandantonio/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/issues/78))

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### Keep Me Section

You can use the Keep Me section to protect specific content like summaries, thoughts, reflections, references, etc. in your highlight files from being overwritten during the import process. (#73)

[Check the documentation](https://mister-gold.pro/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/guide/settings.html#template-keep-me-section) to learn how to define the Keep Me section in your template.

## Bug Fixes

- Add missing backup functionality during highlights import on start (#70)
- Fix race condition when `getHighlightsFolderPath` returns `null` for existing folder during highlights import on start (#70)
- Improve error handling and user notifications during highlights import (#70)
- Update handlebars extensions to be applied once globally not on every template call (#70)

## Refactoring

- 🗃️ Data fetching from DB (#70) - slightly improved parallelization of the fetching logic. Pretty quick, no major performance improvements.
- 🗂️ Books and highlights aggregation logic revamp (#70) - got rid of heavy array methods to reduce time complexity: O(n + m) vs O(n×m).
    - ⚡️Performance boost: ~ +50%: it is expected to have massive improvement for large datasets (for example, 100 books × 1000 annotations: 100 operations instead of 100,000).
- 🖍️ Template rendering logic (#70) - made templates compiled once and reused when passing books and annotations.
    - ⚡️Performance boost: up to +50%
- 🗄️ **New backing up logic now preserves the entire highlights folder structure including subfolders and nested files, not just direct children.** (#70)
- 🔍 Search **"From a specific book" is now almost instantaneous and filters through a list of books/authors without re-querying the database on each keystroke.** (#70)
- 🧱 Better code modularity and testability (#70). The codebase was refactored into clear modules. Tests now benefit from fixtures, easier database mock setup without ORM dependency, and proper isolation, which made them smaller, faster and easier to maintain. **Code coverage improved significantly**.
- 🗑️ Cleanup & maintenance - Removed old db/migration scaffolding and redundant utils/method files across the repo. (#70)
- 🛠️ Tooling: Replaced [Biome](https://biomejs.dev) with [oxc tooling](https://oxc.rs) to ensure consistent linting/formatting workflow both locally and on CI, as well as improve performance. (#70)
- 🧪 Test fixes:
    - Replaced the factory-based `vi.mock` for `child_process` with `vi.mock('child_process', { spy: true })` in `dataFetching.spec.ts` for cleaner ESM-compatible mocking, avoiding the known Vitest limitation with module namespace spying. (#70)
    - Fixed a typo in a `templateProcessing.spec.ts` test name ("formtting" → "formatting"). (#70)

- Streamline import process and enhance error handling with utility functions (#72)

## CI

- Update node.js to version 24 in docs and release workflows (#71)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

* Updated `backupHighlights` and `checkBookExistence` functions to handle custom filename template instead of raw book title during a single book import. ([#67](https://github.com/bandantonio/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/issues/67), [#68](https://github.com/bandantonio/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/issues/68))

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### Book “finished date” template variable

The [list of supported template variables](https://mister-gold.pro/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/customization/templates-and-variables.html#template-variables) has been expanded to include a new one: `{{bookFinishedDate}}`. Check the usage example in the **Custom templates** section of the [plugin's documentation](https://mister-gold.pro/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/customization/templates-and-variables.html#custom-templates).

## Other
* Switch release creation flow to github cli. Add release notes template (https://github.com/bandantonio/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/pull/59)
* Migrate to [Biome](https://biomejs.dev) linter and formatter (https://github.com/bandantonio/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/pull/60)
* Remove husky in favor of using git hooks directly (https://github.com/bandantonio/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/pull/62)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### Template for naming highlight files

This plugin now supports adding custom names for generated highlight files. For more details along with a list of available template variables, check the [plugin's documentation](https://mister-gold.pro/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/guide/settings.html#template-for-naming-highlight-files)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### Refactored book import logic

The main purpose of this refactoring was to make the import processes more consistent and user-friendly.

This update includes:

- Refactor of book import logic that does not overwrite existing highlights
- Dialog box to confirm overwrite of existing book highlight(s) for cases when backups are disabled
- [Documentation describing the updated behavior](https://mister-gold.pro/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/guide/settings.html#backup-highlights)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### Sorting highlights

The plugin now supports sorting of highlights by the following criteria:

- creation date (from old to new and vice versa)
- modification date (from old to new and vice versa)
- location in a book

You can configure the sorting criteria in the plugin settings.

For more details on how to use the sorting feature along with examples, check the [plugin's documentation](https://mister-gold.pro/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/guide/settings.html#highlights-sorting-criterion)

## Bug Fixes

- Removed excessive newline characters generated at the end of the `contextualText`, resulting in inconsistent rendering of highlights in the Editing view (#28)
- Corrected misspelled 'highlight' word throughout the codebase (#36)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### Links to highlighted fragments in Apple Books

When working with your highlights, you may want to get back to the exact place in the book where this highlighted fragment was taken. From now on, the default template features the `highlightLocation` template variable that allows you to specify such links. Check the example in the [`Template variables`](https://github.com/bandantonio/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin?tab=readme-ov-file#template-variables) section in the README.

## Bug Fixes

* Minor syntactic issue in the example for the [template with colored highlights](https://github.com/bandantonio/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin?tab=readme-ov-file#template-with-colored-highlights) resulted in unnecessary newlines in the generated files.

## New contributors

* @absorpheus

* The plugin didn't resolve helper functions when using a template with a custom date-time format (fixes #25)

* The plugin didn't handle properly highlights containing newlines (fixes #21)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

## Bug Fixes

* Incorrect paths to databases resulted in the `no such table <TABLE_NAME>` errors in the console (fixes #17)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

* The plugin didn't handle empty responses from the Books database (no books or no highlights) properly and returned generic non-user-friendly errors (fixes #15).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

* Large amount of data in response from the Books database didn't fit into the default size of the buffer, resulting in the `ERR_CHILD_PROCESS_STDIO_MAXBUFFER` error in the console (fixes #11).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### 1. Add support for colored highlights in template

From now on, you can import your highlights to Obsidian in the same color as you marked them in Apple Books. For more information, please check the [Template with colored highlights](https://github.com/bandantonio/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin?tab=readme-ov-file#template-with-colored-highlights) section in the README file.

### 2. Add support for Date formatting

To set the desired date output format, a new `dateFormat` helper is now available within the template. For more information, please check the [Date formatting](https://github.com/bandantonio/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin?tab=readme-ov-file#date-formatting) section in the README file.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

* A book title containing non-YAML compatible symbols (like colon `:`) resulted in a broken Properties block (frontmatter) in the Reading mode (fixes #3).
* Suboptimal configuration of the default template allowed rendering of escaped HTML entities like `&` or `&#x27` in the resulting Markdown files (fixes #4).

----

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/bandantonio/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin/compare/1.1.0...1.1.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### 1. Add import from a specific book

To use this feature:

1. Press `Cmd+P`
2. Find the `Apple Books - Import Highlights: From a specific book...` command and click it
3. Select the desired book from the list. The list shows only books that contain highlights  

### 2. Add option to import all highlights on startup

> [!WARNING]
> When the option is enabled, the plugin retrieves all the highlights from all the books and ***overwrites the content in the `Highlights` folder*** with the recent updates.
> 
> According to the above, it is recommended to use this option together with the enabled **Backup highlights** option to avoid potential data loss.

## Other

- Update README with a list of all available [template variables](https://github.com/bandantonio/obsidian-apple-books-highlights-plugin?tab=readme-ov-file#template-variables) ([Request from the forum](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/new-plugin-apple-books-import-highlights/76856/2?u=bandantonio)).
- Minor textual changes of the plugin commands in the Commands palette.
